docs.title: Lumina V3 Overview
docs.text:
Lumina V3, codenamed 'Chimera', is a cognitive autonomous trading system designed to move beyond linear algorithmic pipelines. Unlike traditional systems that separate data ingestion, feature engineering, and modelling into isolated states, Lumina V3 employs deep sensor fusion. It treats market data, news sentiment, and corporate structural relationships as a single, end-to-end differentiable computation graph README.md#14-32

The system is built around the "Thalamus" analogy: multiple sensory streams are merged into a holographic latent state before a central Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent makes a decision README.md#34-38

docs.title: The Chimera Architecture
docs.text: The architecture is divided into three functional layers: Perception, Fusion, and Cognition. This layered approach ensures that the agent acts on a unified representation of the market regime rather than raw, noisy signals.

docs.title: System Components and Code Entities
docs.text: The following diagram maps the high-level conceptual layers to the specific code entities and services that implement them.

docs.title: Diagram: Chimera Architecture Mapping
docs.text:
graph TD

   subgraph "Perception Layer (Layer 1)"
      direction TB
      OHLCV["OHLCV Data"] --> TFT[TemporalFusionTransformer]
      NEWS["News/Filing"] --> DFE[DistilledFinancialEncoder]
      CORR[Correlations] --> GAT["GraphEncoder (GATv2)"]
   end

   subgraph "Fusion Layer (Layer 2)"
      direction TB
      TFT --> |128-d| DFN[DeepFusionNexus]
      DFE --> |64-d| DFN
      GAT --> |32-d| DFN
      DFN --> |"256-d + Uncertainty"| SA[StateAssembler]
   end

   subgraph "Cognition Layer (Layer 3)"
      direction TB
      SA --> PPO["PPOAgent"] --> UG[UncertaintyGate]
   end


   subgraph "Execution & Safety"
      direction TB
      UG --> SAR["SafetyArbitrator"] --> BBI[BaseBroker Interface]
   end

Sources: README.md#42-66 pyproject.toml#17-21 backend/config/constants.py#79-80

docs.title: Key Design Principles
docs.text:
1. Dimensional Contract: To maintain the integrity of the fusion graph, vector dimensions are fixed. The system uses a 128-d price embedding, 64-d semantic embedding, and 32-d structural embedding, resulting in a 256-d latent state for the agent README.md#68-80
  2. Uncertainty Quantification: The Fusion Layer utilized MC-Dropout to estimate the variance (uncertainty) of its internal state. If uncertainty exceeds the UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD, the Uncertainty Gate prevents the agent from executing trades .env.example#51-52 README.md#51-56
  3. Adversarial Training (Spartan Curriculum): The agent is not just trained on historical data but is subjected to a three-phase curriculum including Behavioral Cloning, Domain Randomization (adversarial warps like "Flash Crashes"), and Sharpe Optimization README.md#136-138
  4. Hard-Rule Safety: A Safety Arbitrator acts as a final veto layer, enforcing non-negotiable risk rules (e.g., MAX_DRAWDOWN_LIMIT) before any order reaches the broker .env.example#52 README.md#59-62

docs.title: System Data Flow
docs.text: Data flows raw collectors into a dual-storage backend before being processed by the inference services.

docs.title: Diagram: Data Engine to Execution Flow
docs.text:
graph LR

   subgraph "Data Ingestion"
      YFC[YFinanceCollector] --> TDB["TimescaleDB<br/>(ohlcv_1m)"]
      WS["Polygon.io (WS)"] --> REDIS["Redis (tick-latest)"]
   end

   subgraph "Feature Serving"
      TDB --> OFFS["OfflineFeatureStore"]
      REDIS --> ONFS["OnlineFeatureStore"]
   end

   subgraph "Inference Action"
      OFFS --> ENCODERS["Encoders<br/>(TFT/LLM/GAT)"]
      ONFS --> ENCODERS["Encoders<br/>(TFT/LLM/GAT)"]
      ENCODERS --> NEXUS[Nexus] --> PPO["PPOAgent"]
   end

   subgraph "Brokerage"
      PPO --> EXO[ExecutionOrchestrator]
      EXO --> AB["AlpacaBroker"]
      EXO --> PB["PaperBroker"]
   end

Sources: pyproject.toml#40-108 .env.example#12-32 Makefile#127-134

docs.title: Getting Started: Setup and Configuration
docs.text:
Covers environment setup using uv for dependency management and docker-compose for orchestration. Key targets include make dev for local API development and make up for the full containerized stack.

  • Key Files: Makefile#1-56 pyproject.toml#23-36 .env.example#1-75

docs.title: System Architecture and Data Flow
docs.text:
Detailed breakdown of the "Chimera" architecture, the Dimensional Contract (fixed vector sizes), and the end-to-end latency budget (e.g., <100ms for semantic inference).

  • Key Files: README.md#40-115 backend/config/constants.py#79-80

docs.title: Data Engine
docs.text:
Documents the ingestion pipeline. It supports yfinance for daily historical backfills and Polygon.io for high-frequency 1-minute bars. Data is stored in TimescaleDB for persistence and Redis for sub-millisecond feature serving.

  • Key Files: pyproject.toml#58-71 Makefile#127-134 .env.example#34-39

docs.title: Perception Layer (Encoders)
docs.text:
Details the three modality-specific models:

  • Temporal: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) for price action README.md#86-115
  • Semantic: A 15M-parameter student LLM distilled from FinBERT README.md#117-140
  • Structural: GATv2 Graph Attention Network for supply-chain and correlation analysis README.md#141-157

docs.title: Fusion Layer: Deep Fusion Nexus
docs.text:
Explains how the DeepFusionNexus uses cross-modal attention to combine embeddings into a 256-d latent state and how the StateAssembler orchestrates this at a 1-Hz cadence.

  • Key Files: README.md#46-51 backend/config/constants.py#76

docs.title: Cognition Layer: RL Agent and Training
docs.text:
Focuses on the PPOAgent and its 4-D action space [direction, urgency, sizing, stop_distance]. It also details the Spartan Curriculum training pipeline.

  • Key Files: pyproject.toml#17-21 README.md#77 README.md#136-138

docs.title: Execution Engine and Safety System
docs.text:
Covers the translation of agent actions into broker orders via the ExecutionOrchestrator and the multi-state Kill Switch (NORMAL, CLOSE_ONLY, LIQUIDATE_ALL).

  • Key Files: README.md#59-65 .env.example#25-32

Documents the LuminaTradingEnv (Gymnasium-compatible) and the Spartan Arena, which runs parallel trajectories under adversarial scenarios to validate agent robustness.

docs.title: Data Engine
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py
  • backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py
  • backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py
  • backend/fusion/state_assembler.py

The Data Engine is the foundational subsystem of Lumina V3, responsible for the end-to-end lifecycle of market data, news events, and structural relationship graphs. It bridges the gap between raw external data sources and the high-performance feature requirements of the Chimera architecture's perception layer.

The engine follows a tiered architecture:

  1. Ingestion Tier: Multi-source collectors (WebSocket and REST) that stream raw data into a Redis-based hot-cache.
  2. Storage Tier: A dual-backend strategy using TimescaleDB for high-fidelity historical persistence and Redis for sub-millisecond real-time access.
  3. Feature Tier: An abstraction layer that serves normalized tensors to the encoders and maintains the "Online Feature Store" for live inference.

docs.title: System Data Flow
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates how data flows from external providers through the internal storage entities into the StateAssembler.

docs.title: Data Ingestion and Feature Flow
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph "External Providers"
        yfinance["yfinance (Backfill)"]
        News["NewsAPI / Scrapers"]
        Polygon["Polygon.io (WebSocket)"]
    end

    subgraph "Data Engine (Ingestion & Storage)"
        Polygon --> |"Real-time Ticks"| Redis[("RedisCache (Hot Store)")]
        News --> |"Global News"| IP["Ingestion Pipeline"]
        Redis --> |"Pub/Sub"| IP
        IP --> |"Batched Insert"| TimescaleDB[("TimescaleDB (Cold Store)")]
        yfinance --> |"Historical Bars"| TimescaleDB

        subgraph "Timescale Tables"
        ohlcv_1m
        news_events
        supply_chain_edges
        end
    end

    subgraph "Feature Serving"
        Feature["FeatureStoreClient"] --> StateAssembler
        TimescaleDB -. "Offline Features" .-> Feature
        Redis -. "Online Features" .-> Feature
    end

Sources: backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#38-75 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#80-128 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#98-124

docs.title: Market Data Collectors
docs.text:
The system utilizes a modular collector strategy to balance cost and data fidelity. While Polygon.io serves as the primary production source for 1-minute OHLCV and real-time news, the YFinanceCollector provides a zero-cost alternative for Phase 1 testing and deep historical backfills of daily bars.

  • YFinanceCollector: Implements a sparse-backfill strategy where daily bars are mapped to a single 1-minute record at 20:00 UTC to maintain schema compatibility with the ohlcv_1m hypertable backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#187-202
  • Polygon.io: Handles high-frequency WebSocket streams for price ticks and news events.
  • Scrapers: Specialized collectors for social sentiment and supply-chain relationship extraction.

For details, see Market Data Collectors.

docs.title: Storage Backends: TimescaleDB and Redis
docs.text:
Lumina V3 employs a "Hot/Cold" storage split to satisfy both the low-latency requirements of the RL agent and the high-throughput requirements of model training.

Backend Role Key Entities / Tables TimescaleDB Historical Persistence ohlcv_1m, news_events, supply_chain_edges Redis Real-time State state:market:{ticker}, state:uncertainty:{ticker}, tick-latest

The TimescaleStore class manages an asynchronous connection pool via asyncpg backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#83-96 providing optimized methods for batch inserts and time-bucketed aggregation queries.

For details, see Storage Backends: TimescaleDB and Redis

docs.title: Ingestion Pipeline and Feature Store
docs.text:
The IngestionPipeline acts as the system's "circulatory system", consuming real-time updates from Redis Pub/Sub, performing data cleaning (e.g., news deduplication via compute_content_hash), and flushing batched records to TimescaleDB backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#19-32

The Feature Store abstracts the underlying databases, providing a unified FeatureStoreClient. It operates in two modes:

  1. Online Mode: Optimized for the StateAssembler to retrieve the latest embedding from Redis with sub-millisecond latency backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#19-32
  2. Offline Mode: Used during training to pull massive datasets from TimescaleDB hypertables.

docs.title: Code Entity Mapping: Feature Serving
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    subgraph "Natural Lange Space"
        Market["Market State"]
        Price["Price Window"]
        News["News Stream"]
    end
    subgraph "Code Entity Space"
        Price --- OHLCVRow
        News --- NewsEvent
        Market --- SA["StateAssembler.build()"]
        OHLCVRow --> FSC[FeatureStoreClient]
        NewsEvent --> FSC
        FSC --> SA
    end

Sources: backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#47-68 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#171-182 backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#37-54

For details, see Ingestion Pipeline and Feature Store

docs.title: Ingestion Pipeline and Feature Store
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py
  • backend/config/constants.py
  • backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py

The Ingestion Pipeline and Feature Store form the data backbone of the Lumina V3 system. This subsystem is responsible for the transition from raw market events to structured, high-dimensional embeddings. It utilizes a dual-storage strategy —TimescaleDB for historical depth and Redis for sub-millisecond online retrieval— orchestrated by a batch-oriented ingestion pipeline with integrated backpressure and cleaning logic.

docs.title: Ingestion Pipeline
docs.text: The IngestionPipeline acts as the primary bridge between real-time data streams (Redis Pub/Sub) and persistent storage (TimescaleDB hypertables). It is designed to handle high-throughput market data while maintaining system stability through Prometheus-monitored metrics and backpressure mechanisms.

docs.title: Data Flow and Cleaning
docs.text:
The pipeline consumes data from two primary streams:

  1. Price Stream: Tick data subscribed via _consume_price_stream backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#100-122
  2. News Stream: Global news events via _consume_news_stream backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#124-155

Incoming data is buggered and periodically flushed to TimescaleDB. News data undergoes specific cleaning steps, including text normalization via clean_news_text and deduplication using content using content hashes backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#19-23

docs.title: Backpressure and Metrics
docs.text: To prevent memory exhaustion during volatility spikes, the pipeline implements a backpressure threshold defined by INGESTION_BACKPRESSURE_FACTOR backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#49-51 When buffers exceed this threshold, ingestion pauses, and a Prometheus alert is incremented backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#105-112

docs.title: Ingestion Pipeline Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph Stream_Sources
        Redis_PubSub_Tick
        Redis_PubSub_News
    end

    subgraph IngestionPipeline_Class
        Redis_PubSub_Tick --> ConsumePrice["_consume_price_stream()"]
        Redis_PubSub_News --> ConsumeNews["_consume_news_stream()"]
        ConsumePrice --> flush["_flush_loop()"]
        ConsumeNews --> flush
    end

    subgraph "Storage"
        flush -->|"Bulk Insert"| TimescaleDB_OHLCV_1m[(TimescaleDB_OHLCV_1m)]
        flush -->|"Bulk Insert"| TimescaleDB_News_Events[(TimescaleDB_News_Events)]
    end

Sources: backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#37-75 backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#100-155

docs.title: Feature Store Abstraction
docs.text: The Feature Store provides a unified interface for the Perception Layer (Encoders) to access features without needing to know the underlying storage implementation. It is split into Online and Offline components.

docs.title: Feature Definitions (FeatureDef)
docs.text: Every feature in the system is registered in a registry using FeatureDef. This defines the feature's name, its dimensionality (e.g., DIM_PRICE=128), and its TTL (Time-To-Live) backend/config/constants.py#43-53

docs.title: Online vs. Offline Storage
docs.text:
• OnlineFeatureStore: Optimized for sub-millisecond reads during live inference. It retrieves embeddings directly from Redis using keys prefixed by ticker and feature type.
  • OfflineFeatureStore: Used for training and backtesting. It queries TimescaleDB hypertables to reconstruct historical feature vectors.

docs.title: Feature Space Mapping
docs.text:
Feature Category Dimension Source Entity Storage Key/Table Price 128 TemporalEncoder feature:price:{ticker} Semantic 64 SemanticEncoder feature:semantic:{ticker} Structural 32 StructuralEncoder feature:graph:{ticker} Fused 224 DeepFusionNexus state:fused:{ticker}

Sources: backend/config/constants.py#41-74 backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#31-32

docs.title: Embedding Ingestion and Serving
docs.text: The pipeline ensures that embeddings generated by the Perception Layer encoders are immediately available to the StateAssembler for the Fusion Layer.

docs.title: Data Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Ingestion: IngestionPipeline moves raw data to TimescaleDB backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#173-180
  2. Encoding: Encoders (TFT, FinBERT, GATv2) read windows of data (e.g., OHLCV_WINDW_MINUTES = 240) backend/config/constants.py#79-80
  3. Persistence: The resulting embeddings are written to the OnlineFeatureStore (Redis).
  4. Inference: The StateAssembler reads these embeddings to form the 256-d latent state for the RL agent backend/config/constants.py#68-74

docs.title: Natural Language to Code Entity Space: Data Ingestion
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Code_Entities_Processing
        Ingestion["`IngestionPipeline.start()`"]
        OHLCV["`_tick_to_ohlcv()`"]
        RawNewsEvent["`_raw_to_news_event()`"]
        Ingestion --> |"manages"| OHLCV
    end

    subgraph Market_Events
        Raw_Tick_Data --> OHLCV
        Raw_News_JSON --> RawNewsEvent
    end

    subgraph Storage_Entities
        OHLCVRow_Class
        NewsEvent_Class
        TimescaleStore["`TimescaleStore.insert_ohlcv()`"]
        OHLCV --> OHLCVRow_Class --> TimescaleStore
        RawNewsEvent --> NewsEvent_Class --> TimescaleStore
    end

Sources: backend/data_engine/pipelines/ingestion.py#114-142 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#32

docs.title: Behavioral Cloning (BC) Data Ingestion
docs.text: As part of the Spartan Curriculum, the ingestion system also handles the persistence of expert trajectories for Phase A training. The BehavioralCloningTrainer consumes expert_states and expert_actions backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py#85-100 These pairs are stored as .npz files or in specific TimescaleDB tables to allow the policy network to "warm-start" by imitating an oracle backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py#2-8

docs.title: Natural Language to Code Entity Space: BC Pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Expert_Oracle
        State_Observations
        Expert_Actions
    end

    subgraph Data_Objects
        State_Observations --> expert_states_np_ndarray
        Expert_Actions --> expert_actions_np_ndarray
    end

    subgraph BC_Trainer_Entities
        expert_states_np_ndarray --> BehavioralCloningTrainer
        expert_actions_np_ndarray --> BehavioralCloningTrainer
        BehavioralCloningTrainer --> |returns| BCMetrics
        BehavioralCloningTrainer --> |trains| PolicyNetwork
    end

Sources: backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py#59-134

docs.title: Market Data Collectors
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • backend/data_engine/init.py
  • backend/data_engine/collectors/init.py
  • backend/data_engine/collectors/base_collector.py

The Market Data Collectors subsystem is responsible for the ingestion of raw financial data, news sentiment, and structural supply-chain information into the Lumina V3 platform. It utilizes a multi-source strategy to balance cost and data fidelity, transitioning from free historical backfills in Phase 1 to high-frequency WebSocket streams for live operations.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: The collectors are designed as modular adapters that normalize heterogeneous data sources into the system's canonical formats (e.g., OHLCVRow for price data).

docs.title: Data Flow Diagram: Ingestion to Storage
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates how different collectors interact with external APIs and the internal TimescaleStore.

docs.title: Collector Data Flow
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph "External APIs"
        yfinance["yfinance<br/>(Daily/Intraday)"]
        polygon["Polygon.io<br/>(WebSocket/REST)"]
        news["NewsAPI (Semantic)"]
        social["Social/Supply-Chain<br/>Scrapers"]
    end
    subgraph "Data Engine Collectors"
        yfinance --> |"HTTP"| YFC[YFinanceCollector]
        polygon --> |"WSS/HTTP"| PC[PolygonCollector]
        news --> |"HTTP"| NC[NewsCollector]
        social --> |"HTML/JSON"| StructuralScraper
    end
    subgraph "Storage Layer"
        YFC --> |"`backfill_to_timescale()`"| TDB["TimescaleDB<br/>(ohlcv_1m)"]
        PC --> |"`insert_ohlcv()`"| TDB
        PC --> |"`LPUSH`"| Redis["Redis (tick-latest)"]
        NC --> |"`insert_news()`"| TDB
    end

Sources: backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#48-61 backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#179-202

docs.title: YFinanceCollector (Phase-1 Strategy)
docs.text: The YFinanceCollector serves as the primary data source for the Phase-1 milestone. It provides zero-cost access to historical OHLCV data, allowing for the validation of the training pipeline before commiting to paid Polygon.io subscriptions backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#5-13

docs.title: Implementation Details
docs.text:
The collector is a synchronous wrapper around the yfinance library. Since yfinance is not natively asynchronous, calls are offloaded to a thread executor when integrated into the system's event loop backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#62-66

  • fetch_daily(ticker, start, end): Fetches daily bars. Used for long-term historical backfills where intraday precision is not required backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#138-150
  • fetch_intraday(ticker, days_back, interval): Fetches 1-minute resolution data, limited by Yahoo Finance to the last 7 days backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#154-175
  • _yf_to_polars(df): Normalizes the raw Pandas DataFrame from yfinance into a canonical Polars DataFrame with UTC-localized timestamps and standard column names (time, open, high, low, close, volume) backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#69-134

docs.title: Sparse-Backfill Strategy
docs.text:
To maintain compatibility with the 1-minute resolution requirements of the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT), the collector implements a "sparse-backfill" strategy. When backfilling historical daily data, it maps each daily bar to a single 1-minute bar at 20:00 UTC (NYSE close) withing the ohlcv_1m hypertable backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#187-191

Sources: backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#17-41

docs.title: Collector Implementation Mapping
docs.text: The following diagram bridges the conceptual "Collectors" to the specific classes and methods implemented in the codebase.

docs.title: Code Entity Mapping
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class YFinanceCollector {
        +fetch_daily(ticker, start, end)
        +fetch_intraday(ticker, days_back, interval)
        +backfill_to_timescale(store, tickers, start, end)
        -_yf_to_polars(df)
    }
    YFinanceCollector --> TimescaleStore: '"uses"'
    TimescaleStore : +insert_ohlcv(rows)
    YFinanceCollector --> OHLCVRow: '"produces"'
    OHLCVRow : +datetime time
    OHLCVRow : +float open
    OHLCVRow : +float close

Sources: backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#61-68 backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#179-181 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#58

docs.title: Rate Limiting and Robustness
docs.text:
To avoid IP bans and API throttling, the collectors implement explicit rate limiting and defensive error handling:

Feature Implementation Purpose Backfill Throttle rate_limit_seconds=0.5 Prevents yfinance from flagging burst requests during multi-ticker backfills Schema Enforcement _yf_to_polars Ensures consisten types (e.g., pl.Datatime, pl.Float64) before insertion into TimescaleDB TZ Normalization dt.tz_convert("UTC") Standardizes all incoming timestamps to UTC to prevent alignment errors in the Feature Store Thread Safety asyncio.get_running_loop() Wraps synchronous I/O in run_in_executor to prevent blocking the main event loop

Sources: backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#185-205 backend/data_engine/collectors/yfinance_collector.py#69-134

docs.title: Storage Backends: TimescaleDB and Redis
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • .dockerignore
  • alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py
  • backend/data_engine/storage/init.py
  • backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py
  • backend/fusion/state_assembler.py
  • tests/data_engine/storage/test_timescale.py

Lumina V3 utilizes a dual-storage strategy to balance the high-throughput, low-latency requirements of real-time trading with the complex, time-series analytical needs of historical backtesting and feature engineering.

The system partitions data between TimescaleDB (the "Cold Store" and Analytical Engine) and Redis (the "Hot Store" and IPC Backbone).

docs.title: 1. TimescaleDB: The Analytical Cold Store
docs.text: TimescaleDB acts as the primary persistence layer. It is used for storing high-resolution OHLCV data, unstructured news events, supply-chain relationship graphs and simulation results. The implementation leverages Hypertables to automatically partition data by time, ensuring performance remains stable as the dataset grows into the hundreds of millions of rows alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py#26

docs.title: Key Tables and Hypertables
docs.text: Table Name Type Purpose Key Columns ohlcv_1m Hypertable 1-minute price bars time, ticker, open, close, volume news_events Standard Semantic news data time, tickers, headline, content_hash supply_chain_edges Standard Corporate relationship graph source_ticker, target_ticker, weight portfolio_history Hypertable Equity/Cash tracking time, equity, cash backtest_runs Standard Metadata for backtest jobs run_id, status, sharpe

docs.title: TimescaleStore Gateway
docs.text:
The TimescaleStore class in backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py provides an asynchronous interface using asyncpg backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#80-83 It manages a connection pool and provides high-level methods for batch insertion and window-based retrieval.

  • Batch Insertion: insert_ohlcv_batch handles bulk inserts with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to prevent duplicates during overlapping backfills backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#112-128
  • Time Bucketing: get_historical_window utilizes TimescaleDB's time_bucket function to aggregate 1m data into 5m, 1h, or 1d intervals on-the-fly.

Sources: backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#1-227 alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py#17-42

docs.title: 2. Redis: The High-Speed Hot Store
docs.text: Redis serves as the low-latency state-sharing mechanism between the Perception, Fusion, and Cognition layers. It operates as an asynchronous cache for embeddings and a Pub/Sub message bus for real-time updates.

docs.title: Data Flow and Key Spaces
docs.text:
Redis handles four primary data types:

  1. Modality Embeddings: Vector outputs from the TFT (Temporal), FinBERT (Semantic), and GAT (Structural) encoders.
  2. Market State: The 256-d fused latent vector produced by the DeepFusionNexus.
  3. Deduplication: Content hashes for news events to prevent redundant procesing backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#168-188
  4. Pub/Sub: Channels like channel:state.updates notify the RL Agent when a new fused state is ready backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#83

docs.title: State Assembler Integration
docs.text:
The StateAssembler is the primary consumer and producer of Redis-backed state data. It reads individual embeddings and writes the final market state to specific keys:

  • state:market:{ticker}: The 256-d fused vector backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#86-87
  • state:uncertainty:{ticker}: The MC-Dropout variance estimate backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#94-95
  • state:attention:{ticker}: Cross-modal attention weights backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#90-91

Sources: backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#1-142 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#59-68

docs.title: 3. Dual-Storage Interaction Diagram
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates how data flows between the two storage backedns and the core system entities.

docs.title: Data Persistence and IPC Flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    subgraph Data_Sources
        MarketData["Market Data"]
        NewsStream["News Stream"]
    end

    subgraph TimescaleStore
        ohlcv["ohlcv_1m<br/>(Hypertable)"]
        news["news_events"]
        PortfolioHistory["portfolio_history"]
    end

    subgraph RedisCache
        EmbeddingKeys["Embedding Keys"]
        PubSub["Pub/Sub Channels"]
        StateUncertainty["state:uncertainty:<br/>{ticker}"]
        StateMarket["state:market:<br/>{ticker}"]
    end

    subgraph Entities
        Encoders["Encoders<br/>(TFT/LLM/GAT)"]
        StateAssembler["StateAssembler"]
        PPOAgent["PPO Agent"]
    end

    MarketData -->|insert_ohlcv_batch| ohlcv
    NewsStream -->|insert_news_event| news
    ohlcv -->|Offline Features| Encoders
    Encoders -->|Write Embeddings| EmbeddingKeys
    EmbeddingKeys -->|Read Embeddings| StateAssembler
    StateAssembler -->|Write Uncertainty| StateUncertainty
    StateAssembler -->|Notify| PubSub
    StateAssembler -->|Write Fused State| StateMarket
    PubSub -->|Trigger| PPOAgent
    StateMarket -->|Observation| PPOAgent
    PPOAgent -->|Trade Results| PortfolioHistory

Sources: backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#83-142 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#112-128 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#168-188

docs.title: 4. Database Migrations (Alembic)
docs.text: The schema evolution is managed via Alembic, with a focus on partitioning and indexing for time-series performance.

docs.title: Migration History
docs.text:
• Revision 001: Initial of the core trading schemas. Established the ohlcv_1m hypertable and configured compression policies.
  • Revision 002: Added arena_tables for the Spartan Arena simulation runner to persist multi-trajectory results and adversarial scenario metadata.
  • Revision 003: Implemented portfolio_history and backtest_runs.
  • portfolio_history is a hypertable with a chunk_time_interval of 1 day to optimize equity curve queries alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py#19-27
  • backtest_runs includes fields for Sharpe ratio, Max Drawdown, and Total Return alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py#31-40

Sources: alembic/versions/003_add_portfolio_and_backtest.py#1-46

docs.title: 5. Implementation Mapping: Code to Storage
docs.text: This diagram maps specific code classes and functions to the storage structures they manipulate.

docs.title: Code Entity to Storage Mapping
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class TimescaleStore {
        +insert_ohlcv_batch()
        +get_historical_window()
        +insert_news_event()
        +insert_supply_chain_edges()
    }
    class StateAssembler {
        +_k_market_state()
        +_k_state_uncertainty()
        +run()
    }
    class RedisCache {
        +set_embedding()
        +get_embedding()
        +publish_state_update()
    }
    TimescaleStore --|> ohlcv_1m: '"Writes/Reads"'
    TimescaleStore --|> news_events: '"Inserts"'
    StateAssembler --|> RedisCache: '"Uses"'
    StateAssembler --|> state_market_ticker: '"Updates"'
    RedisCache --|> EMbedding_Keys: '"Caches"'

Sources: backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#80-130 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#86-111 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#125-132

docs.title: Getting Started: Setup and Configuration
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • .env.example
  • .gitignore
  • .python-version
  • Makefile
  • backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py
  • backend/config/constants.py
  • backend/config/settings.py
  • docker/Dockerfile.api
  • pyproject.toml
  • uv.lock

This page provides a comprehensive guide for initializing the Lumina V3 "Chimera" development environment. It covers dependency management with uv, configuration via Pydantic settings, and the containerized orchestration of the multi-service architecture.

docs.title: 1. Environment Prerequisites
docs.text:
Lumina V3 requires a Linux or macOS environment with the following specifications:

  • Python 3.11: Explicitly pinned in .python-version#1 and pyproject.toml#27
  • uv: The project uses uv for ultra-fast dependency resolution and virtual environment management Makefile#4-5
  • Docker & Docker Compose: Required for running the full stack, including TimescaleDB and Redis.
  • NVIDIA GPU (Optional): Recommended for the Perception and Cognition layers. The stack supports CUDA 12.4 (standard) and CUDA 12.8 (Blackwell) Makefile#33-34

docs.title: 2. Local Installation
docs.text: The project utilizes a layered dependency strategy defined in pyproject.toml#6-15 This allows individual services (API, Data, Brain) to install only the necessary sub-packages.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone and Sync: Use the Makefile to synchronize the environment with all optional extras (dev, api, data, perception, brain, gpu).

    make install

    Source: Makefile#50-51

  2. Configuration: Copy the template environment file:

    cp .env.example .env

    Source: .env.example#1-5

  3. Database Migrations: Apply Alembic migrations to initialize the TimescaleDB schema (hypertables, news events, and portfolio tables).

    make migrate

    Source: Makefile#50-51

docs.title: 3. Configuration & Environment Variables
docs.text: Lumina V3 uses a centralized configuration system powered by pydantic-settings. The Settings class in backend/config/settings.py#39-108 acts a singleton that validates environment variables at runtime.

docs.title: Key Configuration Groups
docs.text:
Group Variable Default Description Data DATA_SOURCE yfinance yfinance (free/daily) or polygon (paid/1-min) Broker BROKER_MODE paper paper (local sim) or alpaca (Alpaca API) Storage REDIS_URL redis://localhost:6379/0 Async Redis connection string Storage TIMESCALE_URL postgresql://... TimescaleDB connection string Safety UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD 0.85 MC-Dropout threshold for the Uncertainty Gate Arena ARENA_ARTIFACT_DIR ./artifacts/arena Storage for simulation trajectories

Sources: backend/config/settings.py#14-108 .env.example#7-75

docs.title: 4. Docker Stack & Profiles
docs.text: The system is decomposed into specialized Docker images to optimize resource usage and build times.

docs.title: Service Architecture Diagram
docs.text:
This diagram maps the logical system components to their respective Docker entities and code entry points.

flowchart TD
    subgraph "External Data"
        direction TB
        Y["Polygon.io/YFinance"]
    end

    subgraph "Docker_Stack"
        direction TB
        Y ---> |"scripts.backfill_historical"| DATA["lumina/data"]
        BRAIN["lumina/brain"]
        API["lumina/api"]
        PERCEPTION["lumina/perception"]
        T[("TimescaleDB")]
        R[("Redis")]

        DATA --> |asyncpg| T
        BRAIN --> |"LUMINA_SERVICE_MODE=train_agent"| T
        API --> |"backend.api.main:app"| T
        DATA --> |"redis_asyncio"| R
        API --> |"backend.api.main:app"| R
        PERCEPTION --> |"LUMINA_SERVICE_MODE=tft_inference"| R
    end

Sources: docker/Dockerfile.api#44-45 Makefile#83-104 .env.example#54-59

docs.title: Running the Stack
docs.text:
The Makefile provides targets for different hardware configurations:

  • Standard: make up (Standard Docker Compose) Makefile#104-107.
  • Low VRAM (8GB): make up-8gb (Offloads semantic models to CPU) Makefile#109-110.
  • NVIDIA Blackwell: make up-blackwell (Uses CUDA 12.8 base images) Makefile#112-113.

docs.title: 5. Makefile Workflow Reference
docs.text: The Makefile serves as the primary interface for common development tasks.

docs.title: Data Management
docs.text:
• Backfill yfinance: make backfill-yfinance Runs scripts.backfill_historical to pull daily bars for the TARGET_TICKERS defined in backend/config/constants.py#94-154.
  • Backfill Polygon: make backfill-polygon Pulls 1-minute resolution data (requires POLYGON_API_KEY).

docs.title: Development & Testing
docs.text:
• Hot-Reload API: make dev Starts the FastAPI server on port 8000 with environment overrides for local storage Makefile#53-56.
  • Fast Tests: make test-unit Runs pytest excluding integration tests that require live databases Makefile#61-62.
  • Full Suite: make test Runs unit and integration tests (marked with integration in pyproject.toml#171).

docs.title: Simulation
docs.text: • Run Arena: make run-arena Executes a Spartan Arena simulation run for a specific ticker (default AAPL) and generates artifacts in the ARENA_ARTIFACT_DIR Makefile#135-139.

docs.title: 6. Project Structure and Data Flow
docs.text:
The following diagram illustrates how the configuration and setup parameters flow into the core system entities.

flowchart LR
    E[".env"]
    Settings["backend/config/settings.py"]
    Constants["backend/config/constants.py"]
    Temporal["TemporalEncoder"]
    Semantic["SemanticEncoder"]
    Structural["StructuralEncoder"]
    Redis["backend.data_engine.storage.redis_cache"]
    Timescale["backend.data_engine.storage.timescale"]
    Nexus["DeepFusionNexus"]
    PPO["PPOAgent"]

    E --> |"`Settings()`"| Settings
    Constants --> |"`DIM_PRICE (128)`"| Temporal
    Constants --> |"`DIM_SEMANTIC (64)`"| Semantic
    Constants --> |"`DIM_GRAPH (32)`"| Structural
    Temporal --> |"`DIM_FUSED (224)`"| Nexus
    Semantic --> |"`DIM_FUSED (224)`"| Nexus
    Structural --> |"`DIM_FUSED (224)`"| Nexus
    Nexus --> |"NEXUS_OUTPUT_DIM<br/>(256)"| PPO

    Settings --> |"`REDIS_URL`"| Redis
    Settings --> |"`TIMESCALE_URL`"| Timescale

Sources: backend/config/constants.py#40-74 backend/config/settings.py#39-65 pyproject.toml#17-21

Sources:

  • Makefile#1-139
  • backend/config/settings.py#1-108
  • backend/config/constants.py#1-228
  • pyproject.toml#1-213
  • docker/Dockerfile.api#1-75
  • .env.example#1-75

docs.title: System Architecture and Data Flow
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • README.md
  • backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py
  • backend/config/constants.py
  • backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py
  • backend/fusion/nexus.py
  • backend/fusion/state_assembler.py

The Lumina V3 "Chimera" architecture is a deep-fusion trading system designed to move beyond linear pipelines. Instead of isolated stages, it implements a holographic representation where market data modalities (temporal, semantic, and structural) are fused into a single latent state before being processed by a cognitive RL agent. This page details the end-to-end flow from raw ingestion to broker execution.

docs.title: 1. The Chimera Three-Layer Architecture
docs.text:
The system is structured into three distinct layers, mimicking the biological thalamus for sensory integration.

  1. Perception Layer (Encoders): Modality-specific neural networks transform raw, heterogeneous data into fixed-dimensional embeddings.
  2. Fusion Layer (Deep Fusion Nexus): A cross-modal attention mechanism that merges embeddings into a unified 256-d "Latent State".
  3. Cognition Layer (RL Agent): A PPO-based agent that reasons on the latent state to produce a 4-D continuous action vector.

docs.title: System Component Map
docs.text: The following diagram maps the logical layers to their primary code entities and data structures.

docs.title: Chimera Architecture to Code Entity Map
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph "Perception Layer"
        direction TB
        Temporal["Temporal (OHLCV)"] --> TFT["TemporalFusionTransformer"]
        Semantic["Semantic (News)"] --> DFE["DistilledFinancialEncoder"]
        Structural["Structual (Graph)"] --> Graph["GraphEncoder"]
    end
    TFT --> FSC["FeatureStoreClient"] --> SA["StateAssembler"]
    DFE --> FSC
    Graph --> FSC
    subgraph "Fusion Layer (The Thalamus)"
        direction TB
        TFT --> |"128-d"| Nexus["DeepFusionNexus"]
        DFE --> |"64-d"| Nexus
        Graph --> |"32-d"| Nexus
        SA --> Nexus
        Nexus --> CMA["CrossModalAttention"]
    end
    subgraph "Cognition Layer"
        direction TB
        CMA --> |"256-d Latent State"| PPO["PPOAgent"]
        PPO --> PN["PolicyNetwork"]
    end
    subgraph "Execution & Safety"
        direction TB
        PN --> |"4-D action"| SAf["SafetyArbitrator"]
        SAf --> EO["ExecutionOrchestrator"]
    end

Sources: README.md#40-66 backend/config/constants.py#10-22 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#98-111

docs.title: 2. Dimensional Contract and Latency Budget
docs.text:
To ensure end-to-end differentiability and prevent dimension-mismatch bugs, Lumina V3 enforces a strict Dimensional Contract.

Component Symbol Value Code Definition Price Embedding DIM_PRICE 128 backend/config/constants.py#43-44 Semantic Embedding DIM_SEMANTIC 64 backend/config/constants.py#46-47 Structural Embedding DIM_GRAPH 32 backend/config/constants.py#49-50 Fused Super-Vector DIM_FUSED 224 backend/config/constants.py#52-53 Agent Latent State NEXUS_OUTPUT_DIM 256 backend/config/constants.py#68-69 Action Space ACTION_DIM 4 backend/config/constants.py#58-65

docs.title: Latency Budget
docs.text: The system is designed for sub-second reflex arcs. The DistilledFinancialEncoder is specifically optimized to meet a < 100 ms inference budget on a single CPU core README.md#138-139 The StateAssembler monitors this via Prometheus histograms with buckets ranging from 5ms to 500ms backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#72-76

docs.title: 3. End-to-End Data Flow
docs.text: The data flow operates in two distinct modes: Live Execution (decoupled via Redis) and Arena Mode (inline for attribution).

docs.title: 3.1. Live Execution Flow
docs.text:
In live mode, the StateAssembler acts as a high-frequency driver (default 1-Hz cadence) backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#119

  1. Ingestion: MarketDataCollectors push raw data to TimescaleStore (cold) and RedisCache (hot) backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#80-128
  2. Encoding: Modality-specific services read raw data, produce embeddings, and write them back to Redis via the FeatureStoreClient.
  3. Assembly: The StateAssembler pulls the three latest embeddings from Redis, concatenates them into a 224-d super-vector, and passes them to the DeepFusionNexus backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#230-245
  4. Fusion: The Nexus applies CrossModalAttention and a sigmoid gating mechanism to produce the 256-d latent state backend/fusion/nexus.py#84-104
  5. Uncertainty: Epistemic uncertainty is calculated via MC-Dropout (stochastic forward passes) backend/fusion/nexus.py#110-133

docs.title: Data Flow: From Raw Ingestion to Latent State
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant TimescaleDB
    participant RedisCache
    participant Encoders as "Encoders (TFT/LLM/GAT)"
    participant StateAssembler
    participant DeepFusionNexus
    Note over TimescaleDB, RedisCache: "Raw Data Ingestion"
    Encoders ->> RedisCache: "GET raw_ticks / news_text"
    Encoders ->> Encoders: "Inference (Forward Pass)"
    Encoders ->> RedisCache: "SET embedding:ticker:type"
    loop "Every 1.0s"
        StateAssembler ->> RedisCache: "MGET [price | semantic | graph] embeddings"
        StateAssembler ->> DeepFusionNexus: "forward(price | sem | graph)"
        DeepFusionNexus ->> StateAssembler: "return {market_state, gate_values}"
        StateAssembler ->> RedisCache: "SET state:market:ticker"
        StateAssembler ->> RedisCache: "PUBLISH channel:state.updates"
    end

Sources: backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#83-132 backend/fusion/nexus.py#110-133 backend/data_engine/storage/timescale.py#112-128

docs.title: 3.2 Cognition and Execution Flow
docs.text:
The PPOAgent consumes the market state to produce trade actions.

  1. Policy Evaluation: The PolicyNetwork processes the 256-d state to output a Squashed Gaussian distribution over the 4-D action space backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py#20-25
  2. Safety Check: The action is intercepted by the SafetyArbitrator, which applies hard-coded rules (e.g., max drawdown, position limits) README.md#59-62
  3. Execution: If approved, the ExecutionOrchestrator maps the continuous vector [direction, urgency, sizing, stop_distance] to specific broker orders (Alpaca/Paper) backend/config/constants.py#58-65

docs.title: 4. Feature Store and State Assembly
docs.text: The FeatureStoreClient provides the abstraction layer for both Online (Redis) and Offline (TimescaleDB) access.

docs.title: The StateAssembler Modes
docs.text:
The StateAssembler class backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#98 is the critical junction between Perception and Cognition. It supports two operational modes:

  • Standard Mode (arena_mode=False): Used in production. It consumes embeddings asynchronously from Redis to minimize coupling between encoders and the agent backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#101-103
  • Arena Mode (arena_mode=True): Used during training and "Spartan Arena" simulations. It runs the entire pipeline (Encoders + Nexus) inline via the build() method to capture RawAttributionTensors for XAI backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#171-182

Raw Attribution Tensors captured during Arena mode includes:

  • cross_modal_weights: Softmaxed weights showing importance of Price vs News vs Graph backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#50-51
  • vsn_weights_by_feature: TFT Variable Selection Network weights showing which OHLCV indicators were active backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#52-53
  • gat_alpha: Attention coefficients from the Graph Encoder backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#56-57

Sources: backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#39-68 backend/fusion/state_assembler.py#140-170 backend/config/constants.py#79-89

docs.title: Perception Layer (Encoders)
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • backend/perception/semantic/inference.py
  • backend/perception/structural/inference.py
  • backend/perception/temporal/inference.py

The Perception Layer constitutes Layer 1 of the Chimera architecture. Its primary objective is to transform raw, multi-modal market data into high-dimensional latent embeddings. These embeddings represent three distinct facets of market reality: price dynamics (Temporal), market sentiment (Semantic), and relational dependencies (Structural).

Each encoder operates as an independent microservice, consuming data from the Data Engine and publishing embeddings to the RedisCache via the EmbeddingWriter backend/perception/common/embedding_writer.py These embeddings are later consumed by the Fusion Layer: Deep Fusion Nexus to form a unified market state.

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text: The Perception Layer bridges the gap between raw data streams (OHLCV, News, Supply-Chain) and the "Code Entity Space" of the Chimera system.

docs.title: Data Flow to Latent Space
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph "Raw Data Space"
        ohlcv["TimescaleDB (`ohlcv_1m`)"]
        Redis["Redis (`channel:news:global`)"]
        SupplyChain["TimescaleDB (`supply_chain_edges`)"]
    end

    subgraph "Perception Layer (Encoders)"
        ohlcv --> TFTInferenceService
        Redis --> SemanticInferenceService
        SupplyChain --> GraphInferenceService
    end

    subgraph "Embedding Space (Redis)"
        TFTInferenceService --> price["'price' embedding (128-d)"]
        SemanticInferenceService --> semantic["'semantic' embedding <br/> (64-d)"]
        GraphInferenceService --> Graph["'graph' embedding (32-d)"]
    end

Sources: backend/perception/temporal/inference.py#18-31 backend/perception/semantic/inference.py#18-31 backend/perception/structural/inference.py#19-24

docs.title: 3.1. Temporal Encoder: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT)
docs.text:
The Temporal Encoder processes sequential price data to identity patterns, trends, and volatility regimes. It utilizes a Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) architecture, which excels at multi-horizon forecasting and identifying long-term dependencies in time-series data.

  • Model: TemporalFusionTransformer backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py
  • Input: A window of OHLCV (Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) bars defined by OHLCV_WINDOW_MINUTES backend/perception/temporal/inference.py#24
  • Output: A 128-dimensional "price" embedding.
  • Service: The TFTInferenceService subscribes to live ticks via Redis backend/perception/temporal/inference.py#37 and updates embeddings in real-time.

For details, see Temporal Encoder: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT)

docs.title: 3.2. Semantic Encoder: Distilled Financial LLM
docs.text:
The Semantic Encoder translates unstructured textual data —such as news headlines and article bodies— into numerical sentiment vectors. To maintain low latency, the system uses a 15M-parameter student model distilled from a larger FinBERT teacher.

  • Model: DistilledFinancialEncoder backend/perception/semantic/distilled_llm.py
  • Input: Tokenized text from channel:news.global backend/perception/semantic/inference.py#37
  • Output: A 64-dimensional "semantic" embedding.
  • Service: SemanticInferenceService handles incoming news events, tokenizes them using the AutoTokenizer, and generates embeddings for all mentioned tickers backend/perception/semantic/inference.py#55-71

For details, see Semantic Encoder: Distilled Financial LLM.

docs.title: 3.3. Structural Encoder: Graph Attention Network (GATv2)
docs.text:
The Structural Encoder captures the interconnected nature of the market, modeling dependencies such as supply-chain relationships and sector correlations. It uses a Graph Attention Network (GATv2) to propagate information across a global market graph.

  • Model: GraphEncoder backend/perception/structural/gat_model.py
  • Input: A graph object Data containing node features (price momentum) and edge_index (supply-chain links) backend/perception/structural/inference.py#26-31
  • Service: GraphInferenceService runs on a daily cadence, rebuilding the global graph via build_graph_data and updating the structural context for the entire universe backend/perception/structural/inference.py#91-95

For details, see Structural Encoder: Graph Attention Network (GATv2)

docs.title: Modality Integration
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates how the different inference services interact with the EmbeddingWriter to persist data into the RedisCache.

docs.title: Inference Service to Redis Mapping
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant TFTInferenceService
    participant SemanticInferenceService
    participant GraphInferenceService
    participant EmbeddingWriter
    participant RedisCache
    TFTInferenceService ->> EmbeddingWriter: '`write("price" | "ticker" | emb_128)`'
    SemanticInferenceService ->> EmbeddingWriter: '`write("semantic" | ticker | emb_64)`'
    GraphInferenceService ->> EmbeddingWriter: '`write("graph" | ticker | emb_32)`'
    EmbeddingWriter ->> RedisCache: "HSET embeddings:{ticker} {modality} {bytes}"

Sources: backend/perception/temporal/inference.py#66 backend/perception/semantic/inference.py#71 backend/perception/structural/inference.py#37 backend/perception/common/embedding_writer.py

docs.title: Summary Table
docs.text:
Encoder Modality Output Dim Cadence Primary Source TFT Price/Time 128-d Per Tick Redis Tick Stream Distilled LLM News/Sentiment 64-d Per Article Redis Pub/Sub GATv2 Relationships 32-d Daily TimescaleDB Graph

Sources: backend/perception/temporal/inference.py#24-37 backend/perception/semantic/inference.py#34-37 backend/perception/structural/inference.py#42-93

docs.title: Temporal Encoder: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT)
docs.text:
Relevant source files
  • backend/cognition/training/behavioral_cloning.py
  • backend/config/constants.py
  • backend/perception/temporal/inference.py
  • notebooks/02_tft_prototype.ipynb

The Temporal Encoder is the first component of the Perception Layer in the Chimera architecture. It is responsible for transforming raw multi-variate price data (OHLCV) into a high-dimensional Temporal Embedding ($d=128$). It utilizes a custom implementation of the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) (Lim et al., 2021), optimized for financial time-series by incorporating gated residual networks and variable selection to handle noisy market data.

docs.title: 1. Architecture Overview
docs.text: The TemporalFusionTransformer class backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py#240-244 implements a multi-stage pipeline designed to extract both long-term dependencies and local patterns from market bars.

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Gated Residual Network (GRN): The fundamental building block backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py#28-32 It provides adaptive non-linear processing with a GLU (Gated Linear Unit) to skip unnecessary steps, preventing vanishing gradients in deep financial networks.
  • Variable Selection Network (VSN): Performs instance-specific feature selection backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py#82-86 It assign weights to different OHLCV inputs, allowing the model to ignore features (e.g., Volume) if they are currently non-informative.
  • LSTM Encoder: Processes the sequence of variable-selected features to capture local temporal context backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py#288-289
  • Interpretable Multi-Head Attention: A specialized attention mechanism that allows the model to focus on specific past events (e.g., a flash crash 30 minutes ago) backend/perception/temporal/tft_model.py#165-169
  • Temporal Embedding Output: The final state is projected to a 128-dimensional vector defined by DIM_PRICE backend/config/constants.py#43-44
